question_texts.refresh_39841_20260716: Will the US stock market crash by more than 10% in any 5-day trading period before 2027?

research_texts.refresh_39841_20260716:
The US stock market faces a moderate recession risk of 38/100 as of July 12, 2026, according to RecessionPulse, though a broad recession signal isn't confirmed yet [recessionpulse.com](https://recessionpulse.com/blog/daily-recession-risk-july-12-2026). While consumer sentiment remains "extremely depressed" at 49.5 in June, initial jobless claims are historically low (215,000 for the week ending July 4, 2026), and the Atlanta Fed's GDPNow estimate for Q2 is around 1.3% [recessionpulse.com](https://recessionpulse.com/blog/daily-recession-risk-july-12-2026).

Historically, midterm election years, such as 2026, have seen the S&P 500 experience an average intra-year drawdown of 18%, often in the third or fourth quarters [fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/07/13/stock-market-crash-2026-may-happen-this-event/). The S&P 500 has fallen into correction territory 70% of the time and bear market territory 35% of the time during midterm elections since 1957. A "sell signal" was recently triggered by Bank of America's Cash Rule, as institutional investors' cash levels dropped from 4.1% to 3.6% of assets under management in July 2026, indicating potential vulnerability [finbold.com](https://finbold.com/u-s-stock-market-sell-signal-is-now-triggered-banking-giant-warns/). Despite these risks, Wall Street's median target for the S&P 500 is 8,989 by July 2027, implying nearly 19% upside from its current 7,575 [fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/07/13/stock-market-crash-2026-may-happen-this-event/). The S&P 500 rose 0.4% on Tuesday after a lower-than-expected June inflation rate of 3.5% [apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/stocks-markets-oil-iran-trump-ai-6807d21c72974fbac48356f83eeebbce).

**No contradictions found.**
